page: UpCloud STARTER-1xCPU-1GB is an entry-level virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features a single shared vCPU from an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 1.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 10 GB SSD local storage volume. Additionally, the instance is provisioned with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is rated in the top tier, placing in the top 10% with a score of 3832.77, which reflects the modern AMD EPYC architecture. However, due to the single shared vCPU limitation, the multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall into the weak category, representing the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show weak read performance at 211.14 GB/sec for small blocks and 35.83 GB/sec for large blocks, while large block write bandwidth is average at 29.5 GB/sec. PassMark memory and database operations scores are poor, placing in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a highly economical entry point for low-resource requirements, trading off multi-core processing power and memory capacity for basic compute availability. The shared CPU allocation and 1.0 GB RAM limit its utility for resource-intensive applications. It is best suited for lightweight workloads such as basic static web serving, simple scripting, development testing, or low-traffic utility services that do not require high memory or sustained multi-threaded performance.
description: UpCloud STARTER-1xCPU-1GB is an entry-level virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It is configured with a single shared vCPU powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, 1.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 10 GB SSD for local storage. Benchmark data highlights a strong single-core CPU performance of 3832.77 in stress-ng, placing it in the top 10%. However, due to its single-core shared allocation, its multi-core performance, PassMark CPU score, and database operations are weak or poor. Memory read bandwidth is also limited, though write bandwidth remains average. This budget-friendly instance is highly cost-efficient for minimal resource needs but presents clear tradeoffs in memory capacity and multi-threaded scaling. It is best suited for basic static web serving, lightweight testing, and simple background scripts.

tagline: A budget-friendly, single-core virtual server with strong single-threaded performance and bundled SSD storage for lightweight utility workloads.

1.0 GB of system memory and 10 GB of bundled SSD local storage
